items.text: Who normally helps you at home, and has something changed with that person or their availability?
choices: Caregiver burned out or unable to continue

items.text: Compared to before, are you able to bathe, dress, and use the bathroom on your own, or do you need help now?
choices: Independent with activities of daily living
Newly needs assistance with activities of daily living

items.text: Do you have a safe place to sleep tonight if you left the hospital right now?
Are you able to get your groceries, medications, and other supplies on your own?
choices: Able to access food and medications independently
Unable to access food or medications without help

items.text: Have you fallen recently, and if so, how many times?
Have family or caregivers noticed your memory or thinking getting worse recently?
Do you feel safe at home, and has anyone been physically rough with you or taken your money or belongings without permission?
Have you had any new accidents with your bladder or bowels?
Have you had a fever or new pain that might explain feeling worse?
Have you been feeling down, hopeless, or had any thoughts of harming yourself?
choices: No low mood or suicidal ideation

items.text: Do you use a walker, cane, or wheelchair at home, and is your home set up safely for how you get around now?
choices: Home is safe for current mobility level
Home is not set up safely for current mobility level

items.text: Who is the person who would make medical decisions for you if you couldn't, and are they involved in your care?
choices: No identified or involved decision maker

items.text: Assess orientation, attention, and ability to follow commands.
Assess hydration status and body habitus for signs of neglect.
Inspect skin and hygiene for evidence of neglect or pressure injury.
choices: Pressure ulcers or bruising in varying stages of healing

items.text: Inspect the oral cavity and dentition.
Assess gait, balance, and safety of ambulation with any usual assistive device, when safe.
Assess ability to transfer from bed to chair independently, when safe.
Assess vital signs for fever or hemodynamic instability.
Assess proximal muscle strength.
Assess mood and affect.
Perform a rectal examination when indicated.
choices: Normal, guaiac negative, or deferred as not indicated
Melena or fecal impaction on rectal examination
